rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PETER ZIDAN KWAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the "supplement" amended the beginning value of the estate when the personal representative should have filed a Corrected I&A to reflect the correct beginning cash as of the date the death, there was no Exhibit A (the bank statement was part of Exhibit B), the successor-in-interest to Glen Douglas Lee has not yet been confirmed via a 13100 affidavit, it is unclear if the personal representative has confirmed the residence (mailing address) for Collin Zidan Kwan by actually contacting the beneficiary, a certified copy of Letters Testamentary issued to Stuart L. Chan has not been filed, as required by LRSF 14.35 G., it appears that an unnecessary ex parte filing fee was paid when the ex parte petition should have been amended, and the personal representative still has not fully addressed whether Probate Code Section 9202(b) applies (notice is required if an heir or beneficiary was PREVIOUSLY confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc.). Unidentified refunds may not be used in calculating the statutory fee.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANTHONY DYLAN TOM
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/23/2025. Issues necessitating a continuance: 1) The supplemental inventory and appraisal has yet to be filed. 2) The ending balance of cash assets in accounts must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. The statements or letters must encompass, not precede, the ending date of the accounting period. 3) No proposed order has been received or lodged with the court. Please address the above issues and delivery courtesy copies of all further filings and a proposed order to this examiner no later than 12/09/2025 to avoid additional continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BESSIE L. FRANKLIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/20/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) A Court-appointed fiduciary must sign and verify his own pleadings, including any supplement or amendment to his petition. [Probate Code Section 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] (2) The attorney's declaration filed on 9/3/25 also does not address all the issues posted in the tentative ruling for the 7/22/25 hearing. (Additional information should be provided in a supplement and corrections should be made in an amendment, not in a "declaration" answering the issues posted by an examiner. [CRC 7.3.]) (3) Original bank statements have not yet been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] If/when computer-generated printouts of the statements are filed, they must be accompanied by the fiduciary's verified statement that they were "received in electronic form and printed without alteration." [CRC 7.575(b).] (4) A Corrected Partial #3 I&A was just filed, which means the accounting will need to be amended. (5) The amended accounting must have detailed schedules, as required by Probate Code Section 1062. (6) It appears that there was far more cash withdrawn than just the (previously) "unknown" disbursement of $107,290.62 shown in the accounting attributable to Michelle Franklin Please provide courtesy copies of the amended petition and any other papers filed to address these issues (e.g., proof of service of notice of the amended petition showing that notice was mailed after the amended petition was e-filed, and bank statements, etc.) to the assigned examiner by 1/9/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AMY WOO OW
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Court to determine whether it is necessary to extend special letters, which expire 11/12/2025. Issues to be addressed at hearing: 1) Petitioner's relationship to decedent is not listed in Item 8. 2) A written declination must be filed by or on behalf of an individual who has higher priority than the petitioner for issuance of letters of administration but does not desire to act. If the necessary renunciation or declination is not filed, the petition must indicate the reason (San Francisco Local Rule 14.24). 3) No notice was given to the Public Administrator, who has higher priority than the proposed administrators under Probate Code Section 8461. 4) Notice was given to David Ow, who is allegedly decedent's son but is not listed in Item 8. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EMMET PATRICK MONAHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will dated 2/18/1991. [Probate Code Section 8225.] No appearance is required. The Court will waive minor defects with the petition. Item 3e(2) of the petition should have been checked instead of 3e(3) because all "beneficiaries", not "heirs at law" (there is a will), waived bond. The sole beneficiary also happens to be the sole heir-at-law. Some of the other persons mentioned in the will were NOT listed on Attachment 8, as required by the Judicial Council form, but they were the nominated alternate trustees of the trust that would have been established for Mary Ann Monahan if she were not yet 21 years old or the nominated alternate co-guardians for Mary Ann Monahan if she were not yet 18 years old. Since the will was executed in 1991, Mary Ann Monahan must be over 21 now. The petition also lists many individuals not mentioned in the will, whom Petitioner describes as "contingent" beneficiaries, but they did not need to be, as only those mentioned in the will needed to be listed on Attachment 8. Status hearing will be set for 5/25/27. If a petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration is filed at least two weeks before that status hearing date, no appearance will be required on that date.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GERVASIO TOMAS GAYOSO DE OCAMPO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/20/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service of notice has been filed. (3) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) If property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States. [Probate Code Section 8113.] (5) No proof of publication of the notice has been filed. (6) Who is the petitioner? The petition was signed by Maria Angela De Ocampo Mirasol but Wendy L. Smith is the name shown on the signature line. (7) If Wendy L. Smith is the nominee, there must be an Attachment 3g(2)(b) to the petition. (Item 3g(1) should not be answered if the decedent died intestate.) (8) Did the other heir, who would have priority to serve, decline to serve? (9) Wendy L. Smith is missing from item 8 of the petition. (10) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? (11) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 1/9/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
